Our experienced technicians use proven methods to eliminate moth infestations and prevent future damage to your cherished belongings.

Our technicians understand the unique challenges of historic homes and modern residences along the Hudson River, using safe, effective treatments that protect both your fabrics and your family.

From the charming downtown area near the Old Croton Aqueduct Trail to the residential streets near Sparta Cemetery, our team provides thorough moth control services that preserve your valuable textiles and clothing investments.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Ossining home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, webbing in closet corners, adult moths flying around wardrobes, and larvae or cocoons on fabric surfaces. You may also notice damaged upholstery, rugs, or other natural fiber materials. Our technicians provide thorough inspections to identify the extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in Ossining?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Ossining typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of the affected area, and number of treatments required. Larger homes or severe infestations affecting multiple rooms require more extensive treatment, while localized problems cost less to address.

faqs.question: How long does it take to eliminate a clothes moth infestation?
faqs.answer: Most clothes moth treatments require 2-3 visits over 4-8 weeks to completely eliminate all life stages including eggs, larvae, and adults. We use a combination of targeted insecticides, pheromone traps, and preventive measures. Follow-up inspections ensure the infestation is fully resolved and your textiles are protected.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! After treatment, we recommend regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, using cedar products or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our team provides detailed prevention guidance specific to your Ossining home to keep clothes moths from returning.
